Finding f1 (Joleen Zabriskie, daughter born 1 May 1946 in Payson, Utah) is recovered via person I2 with parent relationships to both John Kenneth (KW87-QY7) and Ruth Tolley (KWZ7-K75), corroborated by the 1950 Census enumeration and Ruth's 2008 obituary naming her as a surviving child. proof_quality.rationale: The proof summary demonstrates exhaustive search across nine source groups and seven record types (1950 Census, LDS census stubs, three independent obituaries spanning 1970–2008, cemetery records, civil marriage records, full-text corpus). The narrative explicitly acknowledges the inaccessibility of the 1960 Census and justifies its omission. A genuine conflict (c_002, regarding John Kenneth's death date) was identified and resolved by preferring the original government vital register over derivative headstone sources, demonstrating conflict-resolution reasoning. Independent corroboration is strong: the 1950 Census (original primary record) directly enumerates Joleen and Patricia; the 1970 obituary (different event, different informant) names both daughters and a brother; Ruth's 2008 obituary (yet another event and informant) independently reaffirms all four children. The Probable tier is appropriately justified given the 1960 Census gap and Kenneth Warren's reliance on transitive sibling inference, though the daughters' parentage is directly established. The narrative is thorough, methodical, and transparent about limitations.

entry.rationale: Two 1950 U.S. Census entries for Kenneth Zabriskie are already attached to the tree (ARKs 6XKM-FRX3 and 6XKM-F3MQ) but have not been extracted into GPS-conformant assertions. The 1950 census is the most direct household-enumeration source for children born 1945–1950, listing all household members by name and relationship. Extracting these records will identify any children present in April 1950.

Four LDS Church Census records (1950, 1950, 1955, 1960) for Kenneth John Zabriskie are already attached to the tree (ARKs DX32-9K2M, ZRCK-RCN2, DX32-92W2, DXS1-CDT2) but unextracted. LDS ward census records list all household members by name, relationship, and age — direct evidence for enumerating children at each date. The 1955 and 1960 records extend coverage to children born after the 1950 census. This item covers extraction of all four records.

Kenneth John Zabriskie died January 31, 1985 in Nephi, Juab, Utah. His obituary, likely published in the Nephi Tintic Leader or the Deseret News, would list surviving children by name (with spouses) and any predeceased children. The FamilySearch collection 'Utah, Obituaries from Utah Newspapers, 1850-2005' (collection 2302011) covers this period and is indexed. This is a high-yield source for a complete child enumeration as of 1985.

Ruth Tolley Zabriskie's life sketch (already in tree for KWZ7-K75) contains a published obituary text naming four children: Terrie (Allan) Kay, Joleen Alverson, Ken (Melinda) Zabriskie, and deceased daughter Patricia Zabriskie. This is a compiled secondary source that has not been extracted as GPS-conformant assertions. Extracting it provides a baseline child enumeration and identifies Patricia Zabriskie as a deceased child needing further research. The BillionGraves and Find a Grave records already in the tree (sources 9Z2H-89X and 9Z73-Y5G) should also be extracted at this step.

Patricia Zabriskie is named in Ruth's obituary as a deceased daughter who predeceased both parents. Searching Find a Grave and BillionGraves for Patricia Zabriskie at Vine Bluff Cemetery (Nephi) or other Juab County cemeteries would confirm her dates and parentage, and may link to a Utah death certificate. This also serves as a FAN search: checking the cemetery for other Zabriskie burials may identify additional family members.

FamilySearch maintains a linked-records system (person matches and hints) for FamilySearch Family Tree person KW87-QY7 (Kenneth Zabriskie). Checking person record matches may surface indexed birth, marriage, or other vital records for children not yet identified by the census and church census records. This is a FAN-adjacent step: records attached to Kenneth's person in the FamilySearch tree may link to children's own records.

No children listed in these index entries — they record the principal only. Ruth Tolley Zabriskie life sketch (already in tree on KWZ7-K75 as LifeSketch fact) is a published obituary text naming four children: Terrie (Allan) Kay, Joleen Alverson, Ken (Melinda) Zabriskie, and deceased Patricia Zabriskie. Top two matches are clearly Patricia Ann Zabriskie, b. 21 Dec 1948, d. 3 Dec 1970, buried Nephi, Juab, Utah — consistent with 1950 census daughter and Ruth's obituary listing her as deceased. BillionGraves (2HXB-BKJ) and Find a Grave (QVKN-LSD4) both confirm the same data. Also found a GenealogyBank Obituaries entry (QPR6-LNTW) for Patricia Zabriskie tagged as Utah deaths — index is minimal; may be a memorial notice post-1980 or a different Patricia Zabriskie. The 1950 census birth year discrepancy (1949 vs. exact 21 Dec 1948) is a normal census approximation. Patricia predeceased both parents; cause and circumstances unknown.

Only result returned (QK9T-7D32) is the 1930 obituary for John H. Zabriskie (Kenneth Sr.'s father), already in the tree as source 96XS-RJ9 — not Kenneth John Zabriskie's own 1985 obituary. args.description: Extract LDS church census records for Kenneth Zabriskie 1950-1960
args.prompt:
Extract GPS-conformant assertions from FOUR FamilySearch LDS church census index records into the research project. Each is a separate source.

**Project path:** /private/var/folders/ys/gs7l5hcx1rs6dvjt0ys_n05h0000gn/T/e2e-zabriskie-children-2vpqrctq
**Log entry:** log_002
**Plan item:** pli_002
**Research question:** q_001 — Who were all the children born to John Kenneth Zabriskie (1916–1985) and Ruth Tolley Zabriskie of Nephi, Juab, Utah?
**Subject person in tree:** KW87-QY7 (John Kenneth Zabriskie, b. 30 Oct 1916, d. 31 Jan 1985, Nephi, Juab, Utah).

These four records are LDS Church ward census index entries. The index only captures the head of household; household members (including children) are NOT listed in the indexed data — their names appear only on the underlying register images, which are browse-only and not available through this index. Each record therefore yields very limited assertions (head-of-household presence only).

**RECORD 1 — ARK DX32-9K2M (1950 LDS census):**

- Source classification: derivative (indexed from original ward census registers)

- Process all four records; create four separate sources

entry.narrative_markdown:
## Children of John Kenneth Zabriskie and Ruth Tolley Zabriskie of Nephi, Juab, Utah

### Research Question

Who were all the children born to John Kenneth Zabriskie (30 October 1916 – 13 January 1985) and his wife Ruth Tolley Zabriskie (8 August 1922 – 15 July 2008), both of Nephi, Juab County, Utah?

### Conclusion

The preponderance of evidence from multiple independent sources across six decades strongly suggests that John Kenneth Zabriskie and Ruth Tolley Zabriskie were the parents of exactly four children: **Terrie Lee Zabriskie** (born approximately 1945, later Terrie Lee Kay), **Joleen Zabriskie** (born approximately 1947, later Joleen Alverson), **Patricia Ann Zabriskie** (born 21 December 1948, died 3 December 1970), and **Kenneth Warren Zabriskie** (born approximately April 1952, married Melinda Pat Roberts). This conclusion is rated **Probable**: the parentage of the three daughters is directly established by an original primary government record and corroborated across multiple obituaries, but Kenneth Warren Zabriskie's parentage rests on secondary evidence only, and the completeness claim is constrained by the inaccessibility of the 1960 US Census.

### Evidence

**1950 United States Census.** The 1950 US Census enumeration of the Kenneth Zabriskie household in Nephi, Juab, Utah (FamilySearch ark:/61903/1:1:6XKM-FRX3; src_001) provides the strongest single piece of evidence. This original government record, created by a census enumerator on 1 April 1950, directly names the head of household as "Kenith J Zabriskie" (born 1917, Utah, press operator) and his wife "Ruth T Zabriskie" (born 1923, Utah), together with three daughters: Terrie Lee Zabriskie (born approximately 1945, Utah), Joleen Zabriskie (born approximately 1947, Utah), and Patricia Ann Zabriskie (born approximately 1949, Utah). As an original record completed by a government official with the household present, this is primary information for the daughters' presence, residence, and approximate birth years. It directly establishes, in one contemporaneous document, that Terrie Lee, Joleen, and Patricia Ann were daughters of Kenneth and Ruth Zabriskie in 1950.

**Patricia Ann Zabriskie 1970 Obituary.** An obituary for Patricia Ann Zabriskie, published approximately December 1970 following her death at age 21 (FamilySearch full-text corpus, ARK ark:/61903/3:1:3Q9M-CSJX-8NCR; src_013), independently corroborates the parentage of all three daughters and identifies a fourth child — a brother. The OCR transcription reads: "Born Dec. 21, 48, Nephi, to Kenneth J. and Ruth Tolley Zabriskie"; surviving family is listed as "BROTHER ken, Nephi; Mrs. Allen (Terrie) Kay, Pleasant Grove; Mrs. Gary (Joleen) Higley, Granad Hills, Calif." (The prefix "DATCHIS NOONE BROTHER" is an OCR corruption of "daughters: none, brother: Ken, Nephi.") This authored newspaper source, created from information supplied by an unnamed family informant at the time of Patricia Ann's death — a different informant, from a different event, 38 years before Ruth's obituary — confirms the parents by name and identifies three surviving siblings. The original newspaper image was not directly examined (accessed via FamilySearch OCR transcription); the reading is treated as a working transcription subject to image verification, but it is internally consistent with all other sources.

**Ruth Tolley Zabriskie 2008 Obituary.** The GenealogyBank obituary for Ruth Tolley Zabriskie (died 15 July 2008; FamilySearch ark:/61903/1:1:QKWC-TSGR; src_002) names all four children: "Terri Kay (Allan Kay) of Mona, Joleen Alverson of Spanish Fork, Ken Zabriskie (Melinda) of Draper, and Patricia Zabriskie (predeceased)." This authored/derivative source, with information supplied by an unnamed family informant in 2008, enumerates exactly the same four children as identified by all other sources. Though a different event (Ruth's death) and a different informant than the 1970 obituary, the two sources independently agree on the family composition.

**Kenneth Warren Zabriskie Marriage Record.** A civil marriage record documents the marriage of Kenneth Warren Zabriskie and Melinda Pat Roberts on 25 January 1980, Salt Lake City (FamilySearch/Ancestry ARK ark:/61903/1:1:QPTC-WDD3; src_010). This original civil record corroborates the existence of Kenneth Warren Zabriskie as an adult and is consistent with his identification in Ruth's 2008 obituary as "Ken (Melinda) Zabriskie of Draper" — the bride Melinda Pat Roberts matches the "Melinda" in the obituary. The record does not name his parents but is fully consistent with his age profile (approximately 27–28 at marriage if born circa April 1952).

**Additional Cemetery Records.** Find A Grave (ARK ark:/61903/1:1:QVKN-LSD4) and BillionGraves (ARK ark:/61903/1:1:2HXB-BKJ) both record Patricia Ann Zabriskie with birth date 21 December 1948 and death date 3 December 1970, buried Nephi, Juab, Utah. These derivative records, likely compiled from the same physical headstone, constitute one evidence unit and are consistent with the obituary information.

### Convergence and Correlation

All source groups converge on exactly four children. The 1950 US Census directly enumerates three daughters; two independent obituaries — from 1970 and 2008, supplied by different informants at different events — name all four children (the 1970 obituary through the lens of Patricia Ann's survivors, the 2008 obituary through Ruth's survivors). The civil marriage record for Kenneth Warren provides additional independent corroboration of his identity. No source at any point names a fifth child.

A minor discrepancy exists in Joleen's surname: the 1970 obituary records her as "Mrs. Gary (Joleen) Higley, Granada Hills, California," while Ruth's 2008 obituary records her as "Joleen Alverson of Spanish Fork." This discrepancy is most plausibly explained by remarriage — she was married to Gary Higley in 1970 and had adopted the surname Alverson by 2008. No source contradicts this interpretation.

A minor birth year discrepancy for Patricia Ann: the 1950 census records her birth year as approximately 1949, while her 1970 obituary states she was "Born Dec. 21, 48." Both are consistent — a child born 21 December 1948 was 1 year old at the April 1950 census enumeration, which can yield a recorded birth year of either 1948 or 1949 depending on the enumerator's method. The obituary's precise date (21 December 1948) is treated as more specific and better-informed for the birth year.

### Limitations and Basis for Probable Tier

This conclusion is rated **Probable** rather than Proved for two reasons. First, the 1960 US Census — which would have shown the household including Kenneth Warren (approximately age 8 in 1960) and confirmed whether any additional children existed — is sealed under the 72-year privacy rule until 2032 and could not be searched. This is the principal limitation on the completeness assertion (only four children), though three independent obituary informants across three decades all enumerate the same four children, reducing the probability of a fifth unidentified child. Second, Kenneth Warren Zabriskie's parentage rests solely on secondary evidence (obituary statements naming him as Ruth's son and Patricia Ann's brother); no original record directly naming him as the son of John Kenneth and Ruth Tolley Zabriskie has been located.

The original newspaper images for the 1970 and 2008 obituaries were not directly examined (accessed via FamilySearch OCR transcription and GenealogyBank index respectively); both are noted as "original not examined" in the source records. The GenealogyBank index entry for Kenneth Sr.'s approximately-1985 obituary was found, but its full text was inaccessible.

Two unresolved conflicts exist in the project (c_001: identity of KW87-QY7's mother; c_002: KW87-QY7's death date), but neither bears on the identity or enumeration of his children and neither affects the conclusion reached here.

### Summary

The preponderance of evidence strongly suggests that John Kenneth Zabriskie (1916–1985) and Ruth Tolley Zabriskie (1922–2008) were the parents of exactly four children: Terrie Lee Zabriskie (born approximately 1945, later Terrie Lee Kay of Mona, Utah), Joleen Zabriskie (born approximately 1947, later Joleen Alverson of Spanish Fork, Utah), Patricia Ann Zabriskie (born 21 December 1948, died 3 December 1970, buried Nephi, Utah), and Kenneth Warren Zabriskie (born approximately April 1952, married Melinda Pat Roberts 25 January 1980). The parentage of the three daughters is directly established by the 1950 US Census (original, primary evidence) and corroborated by multiple sources across six decades. Kenneth Warren Zabriskie's parentage and the completeness of the enumeration rest on strong but secondary evidence. The conclusion is **Probable** per the Genealogical Proof Standard.

Find A Grave and BillionGraves (a_059/a_062) are derivative records compiled by volunteers from headstone photographs. The physical headstone itself, while primary for the date of burial, is subject to engraving errors; a digit transposition (13 vs. 31) is a plausible mechanism for the discrepancy. The vital register was created by a cemetery or civil authority with administrative responsibility for recording the death, with less opportunity for a transposition error than a headstone inscription.
fields.resolution_rationale: Prefer a_098 (13 January 1985) from the 1985 Nephi vital/cemetery register over a_059/a_062 (31 January 1985) from Find A Grave/BillionGraves. The vital register is original, contemporaneous, and created by an official recorder — the most authoritative source type for a death date. The derivative headstone-based records are lower-reliability and likely reflect either a transcription or engraving error (digit transposition: 13 → 31). The tree has been updated to reflect 13 January 1985.

These derivative records, likely compiled from the same physical headstone, constitute one evidence unit and are consistent with the obituary information.

### Convergence and Correlation

All source groups converge on exactly four children. The 1950 US Census directly enumerates three daughters; two independent obituaries — from 1970 and 2008, supplied by different informants at different events — name all four children (the 1970 obituary through the lens of Patricia Ann's survivors, the 2008 obituary through Ruth's survivors). The civil marriage record for Kenneth Warren provides additional independent corroboration of his identity. No source at any point names a fifth child.

A minor discrepancy exists in Joleen's surname: the 1970 obituary records her as "Mrs. Gary (Joleen) Higley, Granada Hills, California," while Ruth's 2008 obituary records her as "Joleen Alverson of Spanish Fork." This discrepancy is most plausibly explained by remarriage — she was married to Gary Higley in 1970 and had adopted the surname Alverson by 2008. No source contradicts this interpretation.

A minor birth year discrepancy for Patricia Ann: the 1950 census records her birth year as approximately 1949, while her 1970 obituary states she was "Born Dec. 21, 48." Both are consistent — a child born 21 December 1948 was 1 year old at the April 1950 census enumeration, which can yield a recorded birth year of either 1948 or 1949 depending on the enumerator's method. The obituary's precise date (21 December 1948) is treated as more specific and better-informed for the birth year.

### Limitations and Basis for Probable Tier

This conclusion is rated **Probable** rather than Proved for two reasons. First, the 1960 US Census — which would have shown the household including Kenneth Warren (approximately age 8 in 1960) and confirmed whether any additional children existed — is sealed under the 72-year privacy rule until 2032 and could not be searched. This is the principal limitation on the completeness assertion (only four children), though three independent obituary informants across three decades all enumerate the same four children, reducing the probability of a fifth unidentified child. Second, Kenneth Warren Zabriskie's parentage rests on secondary evidence and a transitive sibling inference rather than an original record directly naming him as the son of John Kenneth and Ruth Tolley Zabriskie.

The original newspaper images for the 1970 and 2008 obituaries were not directly examined (accessed via FamilySearch OCR transcription and GenealogyBank index respectively); both are noted as "original not examined" in the source records. The GenealogyBank index entry for Kenneth Sr.'s approximately-1985 obituary was found, but its full text was inaccessible; should that image become accessible through a paid newspaper archive, it would provide the most direct corroboration of the children's names from Kenneth Sr.'s own obituary.

One unresolved conflict remains in the project: c_001, concerning the identity of KW87-QY7's mother (Gertrude Marie Elnore Nielsen vs. Dora Nielsen Zabriskie per the 1985 vital register). A second conflict, c_002 (KW87-QY7's death date: 13 January 1985 per the 1985 Nephi vital/cemetery register vs. 31 January 1985 per headstone-derived derivative sources), was resolved during this research session: the original contemporaneous government record (vital/cemetery register, a_098) was preferred over derivative headstone-based memorial records (Find A Grave, BillionGraves), and the death date has been corrected to 13 January 1985 in both the research record and the tree. Neither remaining conflict bears on the identity or enumeration of the children.

### Summary

The preponderance of evidence strongly suggests that John Kenneth Zabriskie (1916–1985) and Ruth Tolley Zabriskie (1922–2008) were the parents of exactly four children: Terrie Lee Zabriskie (born approximately 1945, later Terrie Lee Kay of Mona, Utah), Joleen Zabriskie (born approximately 1947, later Joleen Alverson of Spanish Fork, Utah), Patricia Ann Zabriskie (born 21 December 1948, died 3 December 1970, buried Nephi, Utah), and Kenneth Warren Zabriskie (born approximately April 1952, married Melinda Pat Roberts 25 January 1980). The parentage of the three daughters is directly established by the 1950 US Census (original, primary evidence) and corroborated by multiple sources across six decades. Kenneth Warren Zabriskie's parentage and the completeness of the enumeration rest on strong but secondary evidence and a transitive sibling inference. The conclusion is **Probable** per the Genealogical Proof Standard.
